MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
Off Calendar Sep-19-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JACOB SHAPIRO, Esq. of Shapiro Legal Group (Tel: 650-274-0180) for plaintiff;
Atty. KELLI GEORGE, Esq. of the State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance Company's Law Department (Tel: 925-225-6838) for defendant Alise Rose Vincent.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JAIME MARTIN'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge, counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulate form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling, but takes under submission the amount of sanctions to be awarded.

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS, FURTHER RESPONSES TO CERTAIN FORM INTERROGATORIES AND FOR FURTHER PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS:
(1) WITHIN FIFTEEN CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER DEFENDANT ALISE ROSE VINCENT WILL PROVIDE CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTION TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS SET TWO, NOS. 3,5,8,11,16,18,& 20. THE CODE REQUIRES A RESPONSE OF EITHER ADMIT, DENY OR UNABLE TO ADMIT OR DENY.
(2) WITHIN FIFTEEN CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER DEFENDANT ALISE ROSE VINCENT WILL PROVIDE CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTION TO PLAINTIFF'S FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET THREE NOS 15.1 & 17.1. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED.
(3) WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER DEFENDANT ALISE ROSE VINCENT WILL DELIVER TO PLAINTIFF'S THE DOCUMENTS IDENTIFIED IN RESPONSE TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NO. 6 AND IN RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORY NO 16.10(a-b).

COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER CONSISTENT WITH THE COURT'S RULING, AND LEAVING BLANK THE AMOUNT OF SANCTIONS TO BE AWARDED. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A COPY TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY IN WORD FORM TO THE COURT AT RENNEPAR85@GMAIL.COM.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Ellen Bellen, CSR No. 9575, Email: ellenbellen9575@gmail.com, Tel: 707-721-6228 (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions (Part 2 Of 2 For Purposes Of Entry Of Tenative Ruling)
Off Calendar Jun-07-2022 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-07-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JAIME MARTIN's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff JAIME MARTIN ("MARTIN") moves to compel Defendant ALISE ROSE VINCENT to provide further responses to MARTIN's Form Interrogatories, Set Two, and seeks sanctions. VINCENT opposes the motion citing to interim amended responses and also makes a request for sanctions. further After reviewing the moving, opposing an rely papers, the court makes the following findings and rulings.

MARTIN propounded set two form interrogatories in March 21, 2022 and VINCENT served responses on April 25, 2022. On that same date, MARTIN's counsel served a meet and confer letter addressing issues in regard to MARTIN's responses to form interrogatories 16.1 through 16.10. The letter set a deadline for VINCENT to provide amended responses within four days, April 29, 2022. There was no response to the letter and MARTIN filed the instant motion on May 6, 2022. After the May 6th filing, there were further communications between counsel regarding the adequacy of the initial responses and VINCENT served two amended sets of responses, both unverified. In effect, the meet and confer discussions occurred after the filing of the motion. Based on MARTIN's reply brief, it appears that the following form interrogatory responses remain at issue: 16.1, 16.5, 16.7, 16.8, 16.9 and 16.10.

The court reviewed VINCENT's second amended responses to form interrogatory 16.1 dated May 24, 2022. VINCENT's responses to 16.1 is partially responsive. Responses to subdivisions (a) and (b) are adequate. However, the responses to subdivisions (c) and (d) are incomplete and evasive. The persons with knowledge must be identified and the documents more specifically stated. The response to 16.5 is either incomplete or evasive as to all subdivisions. VINCENT's responses to 16.7 and 16.8 are evasive by suggesting that MARTIN is not asserting a property damage loss despite MARTIN having apparently itemized property damage loss in response to form interrogatories. VINCENT provides only objections to 16.9, which the court does not find persuasive. To the extent VINCENT has responsive information, it is to be provided. However, the court notes that it is not aware of any law requiring a defendant's insured to affirmatively search an index to generate a report in order to respond to this form interrogatory. Finally, the response to 16.10 is non-responsive and fails to respond to each subdivision.

Based upon the foregoing, the motion is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART, and the court makes the following orders:

1. VINCENT is ordered to serve verifications to the two amended responses to form interrogatories, set two, no later than ten (10) days after notice of entry of this Order.

2. VINCENT is ordered to provide further verified responses to form interrogatories, set two, as to the following interrogatories: 16.1 (c) and (d), 16.5, 16.7, 16.8, 16.9 and 16.10.

3. MARTIN's request for sanctions is denied due to inadequate meet and confer prior to filing the motion.

4. VINCENT's request for sanctions is denied.

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Spaulding; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
